acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Migration backup publication prunes to the retention cap even if the protected backup ref already existed.
- [ ] Migration integration coverage proves the preexisting-protected-ref case end-to-end.
- [ ] The tests fail if migration leaves backup refs above the cap in that case.
description:
**Problem**
The migration path relies on `ensure_backup_ref_with_observer()` before local store-ref updates, but that helper only prunes backup refs when it creates a new protected ref. If `refs/beads/backup/<current_local_oid>` already exists, migration can leave backup refs above the retention cap, which drifts from the hard-cutover retention contract.

**Files:** `crates/beads-git/src/sync.rs`, `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs`
design:
**Design**
Make migration backup publication enforce the cap even when the protected backup ref was already present, then pin that case end-to-end in the migration suite.

- Update backup publication so pruning still runs with the protected OID when the backup ref already exists.
- Add migration integration coverage for a rewrite path where the protected backup ref pre-exists and the backup namespace is already at or above the cap.
- Assert both the cap and protected-ref preservation after migration.
title: Migration backup pruning should enforce the cap even when the protected ref already exists
Reduce repl e2e per-issue waits to speed slow-tests
